text: crouch . McGowan’s metal legs clacked on the floor as he sank down beside Ramirez. “Samuel, I need you to remove your hand so I can assess your injury .” “Told you…not to fucking…call me that .” “J-just move i-i-it,” McGowan replied, his optics — two large, reflective circles — visibly adjusting. With gentle firmness, he pried Ramirez’s hand away. Fresh blood rose from the wound, visible through the tear in the man’s shirt. Head swiveling on a neck with too many joints, McGowan delicately prodded the entry wound, and then guided Ramirez to sit up so he could examine the exit wound. “Prognosis omitted .” “The fuck that mean?” Ramirez demanded . “High likelihood of perforations to internal organs and presence of contaminated foreign material. Emergency surgery unviable at current location and/or situation .” “There are rooms in this facility that were likely set up for the care of humans. If any of those worked …” McGowan’s optics contracted and dilated. His head trembled faintly for just under a second. “I n-need access,” he finally said . Ronin nodded and stood up. Despite his damaged hip, he ran through the halls, following his memory to the repair room. Ramirez’s condition flashed repeatedly through his CPU, churning up more memories . Memories of Lara battered, bloodied, and broken . Mercy was in the repair room, along with two other synths Ronin hadn’t seen — a male and a female — and the machine attendant. They all turned sharply toward the door at Ronin’s entry . “Is it finished?” Mercy asked . “No, but we have at least one wounded human. Is there anything in this place that could help ?” “Despite their disuse, the operating rooms have been maintained. There is a functioning Automated Operating Table in one of them,” the attendant replied . “I’m not asking any of you to fight,” Ronin said, “but we need your assistance. If you want Warlord deposed...all of us have parts to play .” They stared at Ronin in silence for several seconds, undoubtedly weighing chances of success against risks and rewards. Finally, Mercy stood up . “I believe there’s enough uncorrupted data in my memory to help,” she said. “Take me to the patient .” All four bots followed Ronin. The gunfire’s volume increased drastically as they neared the reception area. Ramirez clung to McGowan, and the bot had his hands clasped over the entry and exit wounds, blood smeared over his casing . “We have more wounded incoming,” McGowan said when Ronin arrived . “They’re going to help.” Ronin indicated the other bots. “Coordinate with them .” Leaving the group to their work, Ronin moved forward, dipping into a crouch to remain behind the cover. The barricade had dozens of holes through it. He kneeled beside Maul as another volley of bullets hammered the barrier. Bits of plastic, wood, and metal fell over the soldiers . “We’ve knocked out at least six of them,” Maul said, voice modulator turned up to overcome the cacophony, “but we count another twenty-plus still functioning on top of that .” “A lot of those are damaged,” Ronin said. “We just need to hold out. Do we have anyone monitoring our flanks?” He shifted his rifle into his hands . “What should I have them watch? There are a hundred damned windows for them to come through. They could get in from anywhere .” The gearheads’ gunfire shifted focus to the upper floors. Ronin, Maul, and the others rose, looking over the barricade. Ronin’s optics detected numerous targets; he took shots in quick succession. To both sides, the soldiers fired as well . A gearhead stumbled out from behind a tree, one arm dangling limply at its side, clothing and casing full of holes. Within half a second, a dozen more rounds perforated the bot, stilling it completely . Movement farther back caught Ronin’s attention. He refocused, and for an instant, all his processes ceased. Even before the videos at the base, he’d been all-too-familiar with the unassuming, unremarkable face staring back at him. The skin over Warlord’s jaw had been replaced, though there was still a scar on his cheek. It was longer and at a sharper angle, the sutures spread further apart, but it wasn’t the scar that made Warlord . It was the emptiness in his optics . Ronin pivoted to aim, damaged hip grinding. Warlord didn’t move as the dustwalker squeezed the trigger . A massive bot stepped in front of Warlord. The bullet hit its broad, blocky chest and ricocheted into the ground, leaving only a faint scratch on its olive-drab casing . “The hell is that?” someone asked . “Compactor,” Ronin replied. The door guard from Kitty’s . Comp loped forward, tearing up chunks of dirt and grass in its wake, charging directly for the front entrance. More rounds bounced off its armored casing. The shots didn’t slow its momentum at all. Gearheads lined up behind it, using it as mobile cover for their advance . “Time to break out the big guns, Sarge,” Ronin said . Chapter Thirty - Four C aptain Cooper took charge, directing the crowd through their confusion. He sent a group of soldiers to escort the children, elders, and sickly to the forward camp, and then turned to the remaining people . “The rest of you, with me.” Cooper didn’t wait to see if they followed. He and his soldiers marched north, toward the Market. Everyone had the choice of whether to fight, but they needed to choose quickly . Most of the humans followed . A high, wailing sound echoed over Cheyenne, and Lara’s stomach sank. She’d never heard the noise before, but her body reacted to it on some primitive level. Her mind flashed back to the scene outside of Newton’s shelter — Ronin pinned on the ground as the gearheads shot him and pulled his power cell. He was facing off against those same bots now, within earshot but so far away,
